sourceMovie.title: Mimic
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Mimic (1997) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 28 Days Later
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mimic clicked for you, 28 Days Later (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mimic?
faq.answer: Try 28 Days Later, The Mist, The Fly—each shares DNA with Mimic while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mimic?
faq.answer: Mimic (1997) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mimic part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Mimic Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
